title: Quantization, Inference Optimization, and MoE
summary: Making LLMs fast and cheap to run — critical for production AI systems.
key_concepts: Quantization: FP32 → FP16 → INT8 → INT4 (GGUF, AWQ, GPTQ)
Speculative decoding: draft model proposes tokens, large model verifies
Flash Attention: memory-efficient attention via tiling
Mixture of Experts (MoE): sparse activation, only k of N experts fire
Tensor parallelism, pipeline parallelism for multi-GPU
exercises: Run a quantized model with llama.cpp or ollama. Measure quality vs speed tradeoff.
What is speculative decoding and what speedup does it provide?
How does Flash Attention reduce memory from O(n^2) to O(n)?
What is Mixture of Experts and how does Mixtral implement it?
thinking_questions: Why does INT4 quantization barely hurt quality for LLMs?
What is the bottleneck in LLM inference: compute or memory bandwidth?
